content.full_text:
CommissioIL 
P,O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322-5662 
Administration 
322-5660 
Carl K. Newton, Esq. 
Burke, Williams & Sorensen 
Suite 3300 
707 Wilshire Blvd. 
Los Angeles, CA 
90017 
Re: 
No. A-82-114 
Dear Mr. Newton:' 
Executive/legal 
322-5901 
June 24, 1982 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
This letter is sent to confirm our telephone conver-
sation of June 22, 1981, in which I provided you with the 
following advice. 
You are the acting City Attorney for the City of 
Downey. 
The City of Downey owns a hospital, the Downey 
Community Hospital, which it leases for operation to the 
Downey Community Hospital Foundation. 
The members of the 
City Council sit as non-voting ex officio members of the 
Foundation's Board of Directors. 
This section has been interpreted to apply to 
reimbursement for travel expenses and per diem received 
from a charitable organization which has qualified for tax 
exempt status under Section 501 of the Internal Revenue 
Code. 
Accordingly, if the Foundation is a tax exempt 
charitable organization, the travel expenses and per diem 
received by the councilmembers from the Foundation were not 
reportable on the councilmembers' Statement of Economic 
Interests for 1981. 
However, after reviewing the facts, I still have one 
concern. 
In view of the Commission's Siegel Opinion, 3 
FPPC Opinions 62 (No. 76-054, July 6, 1977) 
(copy 
enclosed), I have a strong suspicion that the Foundation 
may be a "local government agency" within the meaning of 
Government Code Section 82041. 
I have also enclosed a copy 


Carl K. Newton, Esq. 
June 24, 1982 
Page Two 
of a 1981 staff advice letter to William D. Hopkins which 
involves an application of the Siegel Opinion. 
After you 
have reviewed the opinion and the advice letter, please 
contact me so we can discuss it. 
DMF: km 
Enclosures 
Very truly yours, 
:4U~~"V~~A"--. 
Diane Maur/aftishburn 
. --------. 
Counsel 
\/ 
Legal Division

sections.conclusion: Accordingly, if the Foundation is a tax exempt charitable organization, the travel expenses and per diem received by the councilmembers from the Foundation were not reportable on the councilmembers' Statement of Economic Interests for 1981. However, after reviewing the facts, [FPPC Counsel] has a strong suspicion that the Foundation may be a "local government agency" within the meaning of Government Code Section 82041 and requests further discussion.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether travel expenses and per diem received by City Council members serving as non-voting ex officio board members of a hospital foundation are reportable as economic interests, and whether the foundation qualifies for exemption as a charitable organization.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that if the Foundation is a tax-exempt charitable organization under IRC Section 501, travel expenses and per diem reimbursements would not be reportable; however, the FPPC expresses concern that the Foundation may qualify as a local government agency under Government Code Section 82041, requiring further review.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections with headers; extracted from body text and context. Document appears to be a follow-up confirming prior telephone advice. Contains conditional conclusion pending further analysis of foundation status. Some text is faint/unclear in original.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether travel expenses and per diem received by City Council members serving as non-voting ex officio board members of a hospital foundation are reportable as economic interests, and whether the foundation qualifies for exemption as a charitable organization.

CONCLUSION: Accordingly, if the Foundation is a tax exempt charitable organization, the travel expenses and per diem received by the councilmembers from the Foundation were not reportable on the councilmembers' Statement of Economic Interests for 1981. However, after reviewing the facts, [FPPC Counsel] has a strong suspicion that the Foundation may be a "local government agency" within the meaning of Government Code Section 82041 and requests further discussion.

Burke, Williams & Sorensen Suite 3300 707 Wilshire Blvd. Los Angeles, CA 90017 Re: No. A-82-114 Dear Mr. Newton:' Executive/legal 322-5901 June 24, 1982 Enforcement 322-6441 Statements of Economic Interest 322-6444 This letter is sent to confirm our telephone conver- sation of June 22, 1981, in which I provided you with the following advice. You are the acting City Attorney for the City of Downey. The City of Downey owns a hospital, the Downey Community Hospital, which it leases for operation to the Downey Community Hospital Foundation. The members of the City Council sit as non-voting ex officio members of the Foundation's Board of Directors. This section has been interpreted to apply to reimbursement for travel expenses and per diem received from a charitable organization which has qualified for tax exempt status under Section 501 of the Internal Revenue Code. Accordingly, if the Foundation is a tax exempt charitable organization, the travel expenses and per diem received by the councilmembers from the Foundation were not reportable on the councilmembers' Statement of Economic Interests for 1981. However, after reviewing the facts, I still have one concern. In view of the Commission's Siegel Opinion, 3 FPPC Opinions 62 (No. 76-054, July 6, 1977) (copy enclosed), I have a strong suspicion that the Foundation may be a "local government agency" within the meaning of Government Code Section 82041. I have also enclosed a copy Carl K. Newton, Esq. June 24, 1982 Page Two of a 1981 staff advice letter to William D. Hopkins which involves an application of the Siegel Opinion. After you have reviewed the opinion and the advice letter, please contact me so we can discuss it. DMF: km Enclosures Very truly yours, :4U~~"V~~A"--. Diane Maur/aftishburn . --------. Counsel \/ Legal Division
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter to the Acting City Attorney of Downey regarding the reportability of travel expenses and per diem received by City Council members who serve as non-voting ex officio members of a hospital foundation's board. The FPPC conditionally advises that such reimbursements are not reportable if the foundation is a tax-exempt charitable organization, but expresses concern regarding the foundation's potential status as a local government agency.
